revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八幡宇佐宮御託宣集]]
'''Hachiman Usagū Gotakusenshū''' (八幡宇佐宮御託宣集) is an origin text of {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|qid=Q715632|lt=Usa Shrine|1=Usa Jingū}}. It was compiled by {{ill|Shin'un|ja|神吽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'un|1=jin'un|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119380564}} in the 2nd year of {{ill|Shōwa (1312–1317)|en|Shōwa (1312–1317)|qid=Q1324807|lt=Shōwa (Kamakura period)|1=Shōwa (1312–1317)}} (1313) during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}. The text consists of 16 volumes.
== Origins of Hachiman == The {{ill|Hachiman Usagū Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usagū Gotakusenshū|qq=}}, written during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, incorporates numerous historical records and strives for rationalization. However, it also contains many accounts marked as "One says" (一云), demonstrating the oral traditions passed down through generations. The text mentions the traditions of the manifestation of {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|qid=Q261637|lt=Hachiman|1=Hachiman}}, believed to have originated with {{ill|Ōgami Hiyoshi-no-Mikoto|ja|大神比義命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Hiyoshi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in twelve places. This reflects the extensive development of such traditions up until the Kamakura period. Essentially, the Hachiman origin stories consolidate and rationalize various texts, such as: - The "Statement by {{ill|Ōgami Kiyomaro|ja|大神清麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami Kiyomaro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (大神清麻呂解状) from 815 ({{ill|Kōnin (era)|en|Kōnin (era)|qid=Q1373070|lt=Kōnin|1=Kōnin (era)}} 6), referred to in the "Kōnin Imperial Edict" (弘仁官符), - The "Origin of the Establishment of Usa Hachiman Miroku Temple" (宇佐八幡宮彌勒寺建立縁起) with a colophon from 844 ({{ill|Jōwa|en|Jōwa|qid=Q3577279|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa}} 11), also known as the "Jōwa Origin" (承和縁起), - {{ill|Fusō Ryakuki|en|Fusō Ryakuki|qid=Q11076282|lt=Fusō Ryakki|1=Fusō Ryakuki}}, - {{ill|Tōdaiji Yōroku|ja|東大寺要録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Yōroku|qq=}}, - The "Records of Temple-Shrine Relations" (宮寺縁事抄) in the {{ill|Iwashimizu Shrine Documents|ja|石清水文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Shrine Documents|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
These were brought together and finalized in {{ill|Shin'un|ja|神吽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin'un|1=jin'un|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119380564}}'s compilation of the ''Hachiman Usagū Gotakusenshū''. <ref>{{cite book|和書|author=中野幡能|title=八幡信仰|series=塙新書|publisher=塙書房|year=1985|chapter=八幡神の縁起|pages=24-32|isbn=4-8273-4059-5}}</ref>
== Existing Manuscripts == The following institutions hold existing manuscripts of the ''Hachiman Usagū Gotakusenshū'': * {{ill|Historiographical Institute|en|Historiographical Institute|qid=Q11524667|lt=Historiographical Institute, University of Tokyo|1=Historiographical Institute}} * {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|qid=Q715632|lt=Usa Shrine|1=Usa Jingū}} * {{ill|Hakozaki Shrine|en|Hakozaki Shrine|qid=Q714742|lt=Hakozaki Shrine|1=Hakozaki Shrine}} * {{ill|Yusuhara Hachimangū|en|Yusuhara Hachimangū|qid=Q195715|lt=Yusuhara Hachiman Shrine|1=Yusuhara Hachimangū}} * Others
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * Nakano Hatanō, "Hachiman Usagū Gotakusenshū," in {{ill|National History Dictionary (Shōwa)|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National History Dictionary (Shōwa)|1=National History Encyclopedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6426904}}, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4346755}} (JapanKnowledge edition). * Kamata Jun'ichi, "Hachiman Usagū Gotakusenshū," in {{ill|Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|en|Heibonsha World Encyclopedia|qid=Q5698601|lt=World Encyclopedia|1=Heibonsha World Encyclopedia}}, {{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|qid=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}} (JapanKnowledge edition).
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|qid=Q715632|lt=Usa Shrine|1=Usa Jingū}}
